summary: Large Shrine Route stage/ground decomp PR. It replaced several grshrineroute placeholders with C, added typed prototypes, and reworked the stage GroundVars layouts needed for symbol effects, platform/camera tracking, route state transitions, dynamic lighting, and callbacks. decomp.dev reported GALE01 matched code at 62.06% with +1980 bytes, 6 new 100% matches, and 7 additional improvements.

key_files.note: 971 added lines; implemented Shrine Route init/state/camera/light/platform/symbol logic, added required includes and extern data declarations, and corrected symbol hiding behavior.
Typed fn_80208A38 as void(HSD_GObj*) and grShrineRoute_8020AE08 as s32(HSD_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, s32*) instead of UNK prototypes.
Reworked grShrineroute_GroundVars, grShrineroute_GroundVars2, and grShrineroute_GroundVars3 into offset-annotated fields, arrays, platforms, symbols, and light-object storage.

what_changed: Added dependencies used by the new C, including cm/camera.h, gm/gm_1601.h, gr/granime.h, gr/grlib.h, gr/stage.h, lb/lb_00F9.h, ft/ftdevice.h, lb/lbvector.h, MSL/math_ppc.h, and MSL/trigf.h.

Corrected grSh_Route_804D6A58 fields x1C and x20 from int to f32 and declared additional extern data, including HSD_LightDesc symbols, Vec3 constants, and a local grSh_Route_LightConfig table using GXColor, Vec3, spot, and attenuation fields.

Implemented fn_80208A38 to initialize six route symbols at joints/nodes 0xBD through 0xC2, randomly choose one special symbol, spawn symbol GObjs via grShrineRoute_802088C0, position them with lb_8000B1CC, and apply scale from grSh_Route_804D6A58->x14.

Implemented grShrineRoute_80208D14 as stage init/setup: resets shrineroute state fields, clears symbols, sets stage_info.x6DC to 0, installs ftCo_800C07F8 and mpJointSetCb1 callbacks, computes platform offsets relative to node 0x94, and schedules fn_80208A38 through Ground_801C10B8.

Added a large C version of grShrineRoute_80208F70 covering the main route state machine: route-point detection with Ground_801C3DB4, visited-symbol bitmasking in xC6, JObj hide/show flags, mpLib joint disable/re-add calls, grMaterial changes, grZakoGenerator calls, gm/player handoff calls, stage_info.x6DC/flags changes, camera target smoothing through xCE, and platform translation from camera offset.

Implemented grShrineRoute_80209BEC and grShrineRoute_8020A8A4 for rotating/floating route objects: random angular velocities, damping, clamp constants, sinf/cosf placement, full-turn wrapping, and a reset call when lb_8000B134 reports completion.

Implemented dynamic light behavior in grShrineRoute_8020A21C and light setup in grShrineRoute_8020AA40 and grShrineRoute_8020AB58: cache HSD_LObj arrays, sort lights by distance to the player, enable the closest six, compute a spotlight direction/interest/color, configure 15 table-driven lights, and append an extra light from HSD_LObjLoadDesc.

Implemented grShrineRoute_8020AE08 as a typed callback that ignores states 1 and 3, scales bounding Vec3s by Ground_801C0498, checks the player position, writes grSh_Route_804D6A58->x10 to out, and returns a boolean-like s32 result.

Implemented grShrineRoute_8020AF38 to spawn effect 0x428 at the selected symbol joint, scale it based on the symbol Ground map_id, destroy the symbol GObj, clear the symbols slot, and call ftLib_80086C18 on the player when present.

Updated grShrineRoute_8020B020 to iterate gp->gv.shrineroute.symbols[i] directly instead of using the previous shrineroute2.x108/pointer-walk pattern; also changed grShrineRoute_8020A104 to use GroundVars2 arrays xC8[] and x118[].
smart_moves: The PR first made the GroundVars layout believable: symbols[6], platforms[3], xC8[20], and x118[20] replaced opaque scalars and padding, which made loops and pointer arithmetic line up with the decompiled behavior.

It preserved exact-looking repeated calls and magic IDs instead of prematurely abstracting them, such as repeated grMaterial_801C94D8/HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll calls and explicit mpLib_80057BC0/mpJointListAdd sequences for joints 0 through 0xD.

The camera/platform code stores platform offsets relative to a center node once, then reapplies the current camera offset each frame; that is a good pattern for decompiling stage geometry that follows camera transforms.

The dynamic lighting code uses stack arrays and an explicit selection sort, matching the kind of simple assembly structure expected from the original while still documenting the algorithm with comments.
The small inline grShrineRoute_8020A8A4_rand wrapper around HSD_Randf, PAD_STACK usage, and unused locals show targeted codegen control for matching rather than purely idiomatic C.
The f32/f64 handling is careful in places where it matters for codegen and semantics, including precise radian constants, sqrtf/cosf/sinf usage, and double 2π comparisons before casting back to f32.
Typing callback signatures in the header reduced UNK usage and made call sites such as ftCo_800C07F8 easier to reason about.
decomp_lessons: For stage modules, map gp->gv.<stage> offsets before attacking large functions; state fields, timers, bitmasks, GObj handles, and arrays often share the same Ground user-data region.

When loops index repeated resources, prefer real arrays over fake padding or address-of-field indexing. This PR's symbols[6], platforms[3], and HSD_LObj arrays made later code clearer and avoided the old symbol-hiding pointer walk.

Large stage state machines often need exact side-effect order across mpLib, grMaterial, grAnime, stage_info, camera, fighter, and generator systems; preserving explicit calls can matter more than cleanup.
Dynamic light matching may require direct HSD_LObj flag manipulation, color aliasing through integer storage, stack sorting buffers, and exact distance/weight calculations.

Callback prototypes are decomp evidence. grShrineRoute_8020AE08's s32(HSD_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, s32*) signature follows from how ftCo_800C07F8 uses it and should be reused when searching similar stage/fighter hooks.

Data tables can remain address-named while still gaining useful structure: grSh_Route_LightConfig records color, position, interest, type, spot, and attenuation fields even though the member names are still offsets.
Do not assume numeric config fields are integers; this PR changed x1C and x20 in grSh_Route_804D6A58 to f32 after they were used as scaled distance/radius parameters.
naming_conventions: Address-suffixed stage function names were retained, such as grShrineRoute_80208F70, grShrineRoute_8020A21C, and grShrineRoute_8020AE08, because semantic names were not established.
Unnamed/internal callback naming stayed as fn_80208A38, but its header prototype was made concrete.
Stage-specific GroundVars structs use the module name grShrineroute_GroundVars, grShrineroute_GroundVars2, and grShrineroute_GroundVars3 with offset-style field names xC4, xC6, xCE, xD4, etc.
Partially understood collections received semantic array names where evidence was strong: platforms[3] and symbols[6].
The local data-table type uses a module-prefixed name, grSh_Route_LightConfig, while keeping offset member names x0, x4, x10, x1C, x20, x24, x28, x2C, and x30.
Extern data symbols continue the existing grSh_Route_80xxxxxx and grNKr_80xxxxxx address-name convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: decomp.dev reported new 100% matches for grShrineRoute_80208D14 (+504 bytes), grShrineRoute_8020A8A4 (+412), grShrineRoute_8020AE08 (+304), grShrineRoute_8020AA40 (+280), grShrineRoute_8020AB58 (+236), and grShrineRoute_80209AF0 (+1).

The same bot report listed near-match/improved items: grShrineRoute_80208F70 at 98.56%, grShrineRoute_8020A21C at 91.29%, grShrineRoute_80209BEC at 97.05%, fn_80208A38 at 97.84%, grShrineRoute_8020AF38 at 76.97%, plus .sdata2 and .sdata improvements.
Stack arrays such as u8 flags[6], s32 sorted[20], and f32 distances[20], plus pointer-walk locals sp/dp, were used to mirror original loop structure.
The code keeps explicit casts between HSD_GObj*, Ground_GObj*, HSD_LObj*, HSD_JObj*, u32 handles, and Ground* user_data, which is often necessary in this codebase's stage modules.
PAD_STACK and unused locals appear in the newly decompiled functions, while an obsolete PAD_STACK(8) was removed from grShrineRoute_80209AF0 after matching.
Float constants were written with exact-looking values, including 0.0008726646f, 0.2617994f, 0.17453292f, 0.04363323f, and 6.283185307179586.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
The PR body only said: Tracking PR as usual.
The decomp-dev bot report is the main feedback evidence: matched code moved to 62.06% (+0.05%, +1980 bytes), with 6 new matches and 7 improved unmatched items.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that bring grShrineRoute_80208F70, grShrineRoute_8020A21C, grShrineRoute_80209BEC, fn_80208A38, or grShrineRoute_8020AF38 from near-match to 100%.
Search for semantic renames of grShrineroute_GroundVars fields xC4, xC6, xCE, xD4, platforms, and symbols.
Compare other gr stage modules that cache HSD_LObj arrays and select nearest lights to a player.
Search for ftCo_800C07F8 callbacks with signature s32(HSD_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, s32*) for reusable callback patterns.
Audit any remaining grshrineroute symbol or light code for old fake-padding/pointer-walk assumptions after the GroundVars layout change.
